description: A minimal reproduction filters incidental context from cause.
body:
Removing unrelated services, data, timing, and options reduces false fixes and makes the remaining failure easier to verify.

Evidence boundary: apply only when current task, repository, or validation evidence matches this pack boundary. Do not override a more specific pack or explicit project contract. Use when Code Debugging work touches reproduction steps, stack traces, logs, state transitions, and controlled hypotheses. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include minimal repro, before/after trace, regression test, and command output. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves blind retries, wrapper fixes, broad rewrites, and unproven root cause, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
